title: androsace carnea subsp. brigantiaca
intro: Androsace carnea subsp. brigantiaca, also known as the Alpine Rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Primulaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of using Androsace carnea subsp. brigantiaca include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the flowers and leaves being commonly used in traditional medicine.
